reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: An attacker can trigger this via P2P network by crafting messages with identical unique commitments in different orderings (e.g., [C_A, C_A, C_B] vs [C_B, C_A, C_A]), causing the challenge hash to include different commitment sequences from the original array. This produces different challenge values for semantically equivalent verification contexts, potentially allowing invalid proofs to be accepted. All three FP filter gates passed: Gate 1 (Dead Code) - function has active callers in production bindings; Gate 2 (Trust Boundary) - attack vector is P2P network (UNTRUSTED); Gate 3 (Scope Check) - EIP-7594 PeerDAS is explicitly in Fusaka scope.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: EIP-7594 PeerDAS - verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch challenge computation must use unique commitments
